stories.title: Cubs 10, White Sox 5 — Carson Kelly's Big Night, Crosstown Classic Game 1

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
FOURTEEN hits. Four Kelly RBIs. White Sox streak: OVER.

Cubs rolled the crosstown rival 10-5, Carson Kelly delivering a go-ahead single in the 7th and 2-run double in the 8th. Bregman scored 3 times. Cubs 29-16. 🔥

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Cubs 10, White Sox 5 — Crosstown Classic Game 1
**Verified Facts:**
- **[HIGH]** Final score: Cubs 10, White Sox 5 — confirmed by ESPN, CBS Sports box score, ABC7, Sun-Times, Fox 32
- **[HIGH]** Carson Kelly: 3-for-5, 4 RBIs — confirmed across multiple AP-wire pickups (ESPN, CBS, multiple radio affiliates)
- **[HIGH]** Kelly go-ahead RBI single in 7th inning — confirmed
- **[HIGH]** Kelly 2-run double in 8th inning — confirmed
- **[HIGH]** Cubs total hits: 14 — confirmed by ABC7/AP sources
- **[HIGH]** White Sox 5-game winning streak ended — confirmed (ABC7, CBS Sports)
- **[HIGH]** Trent Thornton win: W (2-0), 2 perfect innings — confirmed by Bleacher Nation enhanced box score
- **[HIGH]** Bregman: 2 hits, 1 walk, 3 runs scored — confirmed by multiple AP sources
- **[MEDIUM]** Cubs now 29-16: pre-game record was 28-16 (from May 14 story history confirmed high); after +1 win = 29-16
- **[HIGH]** PCA RBI single in 7th (consecutive with Kelly, marking first Hudson run since March 31) — confirmed
- **[MEDIUM]** Sean Burke (White Sox starter) took the loss; listed as (1-1) — confirmed in series preview context

stories.title: Carson Kelly — The Backup Catcher Who Just Broke Open the Crosstown Classic

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Backup catchers don't usually break open Crosstown Classic games.

Carson Kelly went 3-for-5 with FOUR RBIs — and the Cubs haven't found a hole he can't fill.

Depth playing like this makes a 29-16 team look genuinely dangerous.

#Cubs #GoCubs #ChicagoCubs

stories.title: Crosstown Classic Game 2 Tonight — Rea vs Fedde, 6:10 PM CT

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Crosstown Classic Game 2 tonight: Colin Rea (4-2) vs Erick Fedde (0-4, 3.77 ERA).

Fedde has a solid ERA but zero wins — Sox just can't score behind him. Cubs erupted for 14 hits last night.

Rate Field. 6:10 PM CT. Take the series lead.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: NL Central Watch — Cards Climbing, Brewers Dangerous, Cubs Leading

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Cards at 25-18 and climbing. Misiorowski just threw 6 shutout innings for the Brewers. Yelich working his way back.

The NL Central is ALIVE — and the Cubs at 29-16 are leading it with a target on their backs.

Feels right.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:

**Verified Facts:**
- **[MEDIUM]** Cardinals record: 25-18 — confirmed by NL Central standings search (AI summary from ESPN/Baseball Reference)
- **[HIGH]** Cubs record: 29-16 — HIGH (calculated; see Story 1)
- **[HIGH]** Jacob Misiorowski (Brewers): 6 shutout innings, 11 K, 2 H, 2 BB vs Yankees — confirmed by Yahoo Sports "Brewers Week in Review: Week 7"
- **[MEDIUM]** Christian Yelich: working back from IL stint (out since April 13); has been swinging and running bases — confirmed by "Brewers Week in Review" with caveat that "could return as soon as next series" quote may be from ~May 10-12 context
**Dropped Claims:**
- "Cardinals won 7 of their last 8" — sourced from ~May 5-6 game, too old to use as current stat
- Specific GB numbers — not confirmed with current standings for all 5 teams
